note:
If you’re still trying to convince yourself that this market isn’t in a full-blown risk-on regime, you’re missing it. 147 tickers flashing HOT squeezes today. That’s 41% of our universe. You gotta be kidding me. Whether you like it or not, the market’s relentless bid is alive and well. Bears? They’ve got nothing. They’ve been steamrolled, left staring at the bull train pulling out of the station. Do yourself a favor—stop fighting it. 

Here’s your cheat code: financials. A couple months back, the world was convinced regional banks were toast, right? $KRE was supposed to be donezo. Well, look at it now, putting up a 98.2 on the daily squeeze with a 93.2 weekly. That’s leadership. Pair that with $JPM at 98.1 daily and $BAC right behind at 98.0, and it’s clear—money is rotating into banks again. The market is telling us something. Are we listening? Look at $PEP making waves too, riding the same bullish regime. It’s a consumer staples name, sure, but it’s also got that defensive-offensive vibe to it. These aren’t your granddaddy’s Coke and Pepsi plays of yore. This is what stealthy rotation into relative strength looks like. 

And let’s not overlook the uranium trade. $CCJ is an absolute unit at 97.1 daily and a ridiculous 95.5 weekly in a bullish regime. Now pair that with our portfolio exposure in $UUUU and $UEC. Both have squeezes >92 and are screaming for attention. If we’re gonna power this bull run with the energy trade, the nuclear sub-sector might be the one pulling the weight. $UUUU’s already given us a nice double, and it’s still primed for more. Need I say it again? That’s leadership.

Speaking of the portfolio, we’ve got CIFR cooking with gas. Already locked in a double, but it’s still throwing off a 93.3 squeeze in a bullish regime. If you missed it the first time, it’s still actionable. The same goes for $UAMY and $MP, both sending strong signals even after delivering gains. These aren’t “what-ifs”—these are booked wins asking for second chances. Are you taking them? 

As for today, keep your eyes on the financials and energy. $KRE is driving the bus for the regionals, and the uranium names are quietly stacking momentum. Don’t sleep on $BAC either—it’s still a HOT setup and might get its breakout sooner than later. Also, watch that $UNG squeeze at 97.6 on the daily. Nat gas seems to be waking up, and you don’t want to be late to that potential party. 

Risk is on. Full stop.
